name: rad-session
description:
Make AI coding sessions remember each other — across machines, across agents (Claude + Codex), across days. Three commands manage the lifecycle:

- **`/startup`** — orients you in under five seconds in steady state. On first run for a project, auto-bootstraps: stack detection, agent_scope question, operating manual scaffold, `docs/status.md` scaffold, `.rad/profile` creation, configs per scope, plugin-bloat audit.
- **`/wrapup`** — refuses if no work changed since last status. Writes `docs/status.md` from **evidence** (git diff, test output, plan-task state), not chat memory. Detects candidate decisions, runs cross-doc validators, prunes the operating manual's Operational sections, archives shipped milestones, and prompts to commit + push.
- **`/add-resource`** — registers a new MCP server, CLI tool, script, or note in the operating manual's Resources section.

Built around a canonical doc set aligned with published OpenAI/Anthropic project-structure research: operating manual (CLAUDE.md and/or AGENTS.md per agent scope), `docs/status.md` (evidence-based reality), `docs/planning/current.md` (intent — owned by rad-planner), `docs/planning/archive/`, `docs/decisions/`.

**Sectioned-writer rule.** rad-planner owns the operating manual's Constitution sections; rad-session owns the Operational sections. Each plugin parses sections, modifies only what it owns, and preserves user-added sections. **v5.4** recognizes the new `Lanes` Constitution section (rad-planner v4.7+ role-separation contract) and treats it as never-prunable.

**v5.5 — compact milestone contract render at `/startup`.** When `docs/planning/current.md` contains a `### Session contract` sub-section (written by rad-planner v4.8+), `/startup` renders it as the first user-visible block after the v5.3 floor line. The 7-field build-readiness contract (Current milestone / Goal / In scope / Out of scope / Files likely touched / Acceptance criteria / Stop and ask if) lands in the agent's context window before the first tool call, closing the anti-drift loop. Graceful skip when the sub-section is absent or `current.md` doesn't exist.

**Cross-machine continuity.** `/wrapup` auto-commits status, archives, ADRs, and the operating manual; `/startup` fetches and prompts to pull. PC ↔ GitHub ↔ laptop stays in sync.

**Permission-mode safety.** Operations with asymmetric downside refuse to silently auto-execute under autonomous permission modes — the `/startup` data-loss guard rail prompts regardless of Auto / Bypass / `--non-interactive`. Operations with reversible downside (draft ADRs) auto-capture so nothing is lost, surfaced for review in the next briefing.

Never `git add -A`. Never force-pushes. Bails cleanly on diverged history. Pairs with rad-planner; works standalone.
